title: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Cheat Sheet
description: Essential concepts and patterns for Domain-Driven Design (DDD).

commands.command: Core Domain: The area of the business where the most value is derived.
commands.description: Define the core domain of the business.
commands.scenario: Define the core domain of the business.

commands.command: Subdomain: A distinct part of the core domain with its own logic.
commands.description: Identify subdomains within the core domain.
commands.scenario: Identify subdomains within the core domain.

commands.command: Bounded Context: A boundary within which a particular model is defined and applicable.
commands.description: Understand the concept of a bounded context.
commands.scenario: Understand the concept of a bounded context.

commands.command: Ubiquitous Language: A common language used by developers and domain experts.
commands.description: Define a ubiquitous language for the team.
commands.scenario: Define a ubiquitous language for the team.

commands.command: Context Map: A diagram showing relationships between bounded contexts.
commands.description: Create a context map to visualize bounded contexts.
commands.scenario: Create a context map to visualize bounded contexts.

commands.command: Domain Event: A significant change in the state of the domain.
commands.description: Identify and manage domain events.
Implement entities to represent domain objects.
commands.scenario: Implement entities to represent domain objects.

commands.description: Use value objects for immutable data.
commands.scenario: Use value objects for immutable data.

commands.description: Define aggregates to enforce invariants.
Use repositories to handle data access.
commands.scenario: Use repositories to handle data access.

commands.description: Apply factories for complex object creation.
commands.scenario: Apply factories for complex object creation.

commands.description: Implement services for domain logic.

commands.command: Anti-Corruption Layer: A boundary that translates between systems to avoid corruption.
commands.description: Use anti-corruption layers to protect the domain.
commands.scenario: Use anti-corruption layers to protect the domain.

commands.command: Hexagonal Architecture: A design pattern for creating flexible and scalable applications.
commands.description: Apply the hexagonal architecture for flexibility.
commands.scenario: Apply the hexagonal architecture for flexibility.

commands.command: CQRS: Command Query Responsibility Segregation, a pattern to separate read and write operations.
commands.description: Utilize CQRS for separating read and write models.
commands.scenario: Utilize CQRS for separating read and write models.

commands.command: Invariant Enforcement
commands.description: The set of rules that must always be true within an Aggregate to maintain data integrity during state transitions.

commands.command: Policy (Domain Policy)
commands.description: A rule that defines how the domain reacts to specific Domain Events, often implemented as a listener or a reaction mechanism.

commands.command: Side-Effect-Free Functions
commands.description: A pattern for Value Objects and Domain Services where operations return a new instance instead of mutating the current state, ensuring predictability.

commands.command: Saga Pattern
commands.description: A mechanism to manage long-running business processes that span multiple Bounded Contexts, ensuring eventual consistency via distributed transactions.

commands.command: Read Model (Projection)
commands.description: A specialized data structure optimized for queries in CQRS, often denormalized to provide high-performance views of the domain state.
